preamble: A glide hardens and drifts toward a voiceless sibilant. This transition is most famously documented in modern dialects of South American Spanish, where it represents a radical shift in the sound of the entire language.

examples.note: Development of the palatal glide into /ʒ/ (zheísmo) and then /ʃ/ (sheísmo) in Argentina and Uruguay.
Fortition of /j/ to /ʃ/ in certain Aegean dialects.

sources: Hualde, J. I. (2005). The Sounds of Spanish.
Newton, B. (1972). The Generative Interpretation of Dialect.
